dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3685 IS: No Taxpayer Funds for Corporate Investment in Venezuelan Oil Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Bennet (for himself, Mr. Welch, Mr. Kaine, Mr. Van Hollen, Mr. Reed, Mr. Hickenlooper, and Mr. Schiff) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs

form.official-title: To prohibit appropriations of funds for the payment of reimbursements for capital expenditures in the oil and gas sector, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No Taxpayer Funds for Corporate Investment in Venezuelan Oil Act.

section.header: No payment of reimbursements for qualified capital expenditures

paragraph.text: The term capital expenditure means any amount paid for new buildings or for permanent improvements or betterments made to increase the value of any property or estate.

subparagraph.text: any United States citizen or alien admitted for permanent residence in the United States; and
any foreign or domestic corporation, partnership, or other organization.

paragraph.text: The term qualified capital expenditure means a capital expenditure made in the oil and gas sector located in the Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela, or a legal successor.

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no funds may be obligated, expended, or otherwise made available from the Treasury of the United States or any account owned, controlled, or accessible by the United States or a person acting on behalf of the United States for the payment of reimbursements for qualified capital expenditures by a person.
